title: dryopteris supina
intro: Dryopteris supina, also known as the eastern wood fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of D. supina include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against cell damage. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
